question: Who proposed removing SSLv3 support and was there opposition?

snippets.claim_text: RESOLVED: deprecate SSLv3 in 2.4, remove in 2.6 — vote +2/-0/abstain 0
snippets.excerpt: RESOLVED: deprecate SSLv3 in 2.4, remove in 2.6. Vote: +2, -0, abstain 0.

snippets.claim_text: Justin Erenkrantz supports deprecating SSLv3, casts +1 vote
snippets.excerpt:
I propose we remove SSLv3 support from mod_ssl entirely.
It is obsolete and CVE-2014-3566 (POODLE) demonstrates why.
Casting my vote: +1

snippets.claim_text: William Rowe opposes immediate removal; proposes staged 2-release deprecation warning
snippets.excerpt:
-1 for now. Some enterprise customers still require SSLv3.
We should deprecate first with a 2-release warning, not remove.

snippets.claim_text: Greg Stein supports deprecating SSLv3 (+1 vote)
snippets.excerpt: +1 from me as well. Long overdue.

snippets.claim_text: Decision targets SSLv3 protocol (vulnerable to POODLE)
snippets.excerpt:
I propose we remove SSLv3 support from mod_ssl entirely.
It is obsolete and CVE-2014-3566 (POODLE) demonstrates why.

citations.subject: Re: [VOTE] Remove SSLv3 support from mod_ssl

Re: [VOTE] Remove SSLv3 support from mod_ssl

ambiguities: CONFLICT (resolved): William Rowe's -1 opposition [c3, valid_to=2003-01-15] was superseded by the amended RESOLVED [c4]. Resolution: b_wins. Both claims are stored; c3.valid_to is set.
